title: New York Fed Statement on Close Cooperation with Iraq Audit Body

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York issued a statement on July 1, 2011, affirming its close cooperation with the Special Inspector General for Iraq Reconstruction (SIGIR) in auditing the Development Fund for Iraq (DFI). The statement notes that release of foreign central bank account information requires account holder approval, which the NY Fed states it has routinely obtained for prior DFI audits. The statement references historical cash shipments from the NY Fed to Iraq during the Coalition Provisional Authority period as matters of public record.
key_points: The NY Fed cooperated closely with SIGIR audits of the Development Fund for Iraq
Release of foreign central bank account information requires approval of the account holder
The NY Fed states it has routinely obtained account holder approval in earlier DFI audits
Cash shipments from the NY Fed to Iraq during the CPA period are described as a matter of public record
SIGIR and the NY Fed expected to continue cooperating on future DFI account activity audits
why_it_matters: This statement is not relevant to market structure or macro catalysts. It is an administrative disclosure about historical audit cooperation related to Iraq reconstruction funds, with no bearing on current monetary policy, market operations, or financial market structure.
